# seerr_notification_email (Resource)

Manage Seerr email notification settings via /api/v1/settings/notifications/email.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `email` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--email))
- `embed_poster` (Boolean)
- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `notification_types` (Set of String) Set of notification event types. Valid values include `MEDIA_PENDING`, `MEDIA_APPROVED`, `MEDIA_AVAILABLE`, `MEDIA_FAILED`, `MEDIA_DECLINED`, `MEDIA_AUTO_APPROVED`, `ISSUE_CREATED`, `ISSUE_COMMENT`, `ISSUE_RESOLVED`, `ISSUE_REOPENED`, `MEDIA_AUTO_REQUESTED`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedatt--email"></a>
### Nested Schema for `email`

Required:

- `email_from` (String)
- `sender_name` (String)
- `smtp_host` (String)
- `smtp_port` (Number)

Optional:

- `allow_self_signed` (Boolean)
- `auth_pass` (String, Sensitive)
- `auth_user` (String)
- `ignore_tls` (Boolean)
- `pgp_password` (String, Sensitive)
- `pgp_private_key` (String, Sensitive)
- `require_tls` (Boolean)
- `secure` (Boolean)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# In Terraform 1.5.0 and later, use an import block to import seerr_notification_email. For example:
#
# import {
#   to = seerr_notification_email.example
#   id = "email"
# }

# Otherwise, use the terraform import command:
terraform import seerr_notification_email.example email
```
